title: Frangula saxatilis
taxonomy: Frangula saxatilis is classified within the family Rhamnaceae. This family comprises flowering plants commonly known as buckthorns or dogwoods. Frangula saxatilis is a species of buckthorn that is native to the Mediterranean region and parts of Europe. It is a deciduous shrub or small tree with small, white flowers and greenish-yellow berries. The classification of Frangula saxatilis further places it within the genus Frangula, which contains several species of buckthorns.
uses: Frangula saxatilis, also known as rock alder or rock buckthorn, has been traditionally used in herbal medicine for its purgative properties. The plant's bark contains anthraquinone glycosides, which are responsible for its laxative effects. It has been used to treat constipation and other gastrointestinal issues, often in combination with other herbs. However, its use should be approached with caution due to potential side effects, such as abdominal cramps and diarrhea. In some traditional medicine systems, the plant is also used to treat fever and other infections.
benefits: Frangula saxatilis, also known as Rock Alder or Rock Buckthorn, has been traditionally used in folk medicine for its potential health benefits. The plant's bark and leaves are said to have antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties. It is believed to have antioxidant and antiviral effects, which may help protect against infections and promote overall well-being. Some studies suggest that Frangula saxatilis may have a positive impact on digestive health and could be used to treat certain gastrointestinal issues. However, more research is needed to confirm the plant's full range of health benefits and potential uses.

Its bark contains anthraquinone glycosides, which have been traditionally used to treat digestive issues and skin problems. Some studies suggest that extracts from Frangula saxatilis may exhibit anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial activities. However, more research is needed to fully understand its therapeutic potential. The plant's active constituents have been studied for their potential medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial activities. intro: Frangula saxatilis, also known as buckthorn or alder buckthorn, is a deciduous shrub or small tree native to Europe and Western Asia, characterized by its thorny branches and small, yellowish-green flowers. The plant has been traditionally used for its potential health benefits, which include treating digestive issues such as constipation and diarrhea due to its anthraquinone content, which acts as a laxative. The bioactive constituents of Frangula saxatilis include anthraquinone glycosides, flavonoids, and alkaloids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. Traditionally, Frangula saxatilis is used in herbal preparations such as teas, tinctures, and infusions to support digestive health, and it is also used in combination with other herbs to enhance their therapeutic effects.
